key_insight: Ultrafast high-temperature sintering can lock metastable zirconia phases into copper cermets while preserving a fine, homogeneous microstructure.
engineering_relevance: This enables tougher, multifunctional copper-based composites without the grain coarsening and constraint loss typical of conventional high-temperature sintering.
method: Joule-heat the compact to about 900 °C at roughly 100 °C/s
Hold only 20 s to trigger rapid densification
Use UHS to favor metastable zirconia phase retention and limit copper grain growth
evidence_signal: The abstract reports cermets with up to 50 wt% metastable zirconia at room temperature, together with fine-grained and homogeneous microstructure.
watch_item: Phase retention and microstructure benefits may depend on precise UHS heating rate, peak temperature, and sample geometry, so process control and scale-up need careful validation.
paper_title: Leveraging rapid sintering to retain metastable zirconia in copper
